included_relationships.notes: Self covers employee's own serious health condition making them unable to perform job functions; spouse, son or daughter, and parent covered for care leave

conditions.notes: Includes any period of incapacity or subsequent treatment connected to the inpatient stay
conditions.source_text: Inpatient care means an overnight stay in a hospital, hospice, or residential medical care facility, including any period of incapacity or any subsequent treatment in connection with such inpatient care.

required_value.unit: days after first day of incapacity
days from first day of incapacity

conditions.notes: Extenuating circumstances may excuse failure to complete second visit within 30 days
conditions.source_text: A period of incapacity of more than three consecutive, full calendar days, and any subsequent treatment or period of incapacity relating to the same condition, that also involves: Treatment two or more times, within 30 days of the first day of incapacity... or Treatment by a health care provider on at least one occasion, which results in a regimen of continuing treatment.

conditions.notes: Qualifies even without 3-day incapacity threshold or treatment visit
conditions.source_text: Any period of incapacity due to pregnancy, or for prenatal care.

conditions.notes: Examples include asthma, diabetes, epilepsy; qualifies even without 3-day incapacity or treatment during absence
conditions.source_text: Any period of incapacity or treatment for such incapacity due to a chronic serious health condition. A chronic serious health condition is one which: Requires periodic visits (defined as at least twice a year)... Continues over an extended period of time... and May cause episodic rather than a continuing period of incapacity.

required_value.field: continuing supervision by health care provider

conditions.notes: Active treatment not required; examples include Alzheimer's, severe stroke, terminal disease
conditions.source_text: A period of incapacity which is permanent or long-term due to a condition for which treatment may not be effective. The employee or family member must be under the continuing supervision of, but need not be receiving active treatment by, a health care provider.

required_value.field: multiple treatments by or on referral of health care provider
restorative surgery after accident or injury

conditions.notes: Examples of likely-incapacity conditions: cancer, severe arthritis, kidney disease
conditions.source_text: Any period of absence to receive multiple treatments (including any period of recovery therefrom) by a health care provider or by a provider of health care services under orders of, or on referral by, a health care provider, for: Restorative surgery after an accident or other injury; or A condition that would likely result in a period of incapacity of more than three consecutive, full calendar days in the absence of medical intervention or treatment.

required_value: health care services provider on referral

conditions.notes: Absence due to substance use itself does not qualify; only treatment qualifies
conditions.source_text: Substance abuse may be a serious health condition if the conditions of §§ 825.113 through 825.115 are met. However, FMLA leave may only be taken for treatment for substance abuse by a health care provider or by a provider of health care services on referral by a health care provider.

conditions.field: employee unable to perform job functions

required_value: unable to perform any essential function

conditions.notes: Employee absent to receive medical treatment is considered unable to perform essential functions during absence; applies to self only per 825.112(a)(4)
conditions.source_text: An employee is unable to perform the functions of the position where the health care provider finds that the employee is unable to work at all or is unable to perform any one of the essential functions of the employee's position.

relationship_issues: API1 uses 'child' in included_relationships but 825.112(a)(3) uses 'son or daughter'; label should match regulation wording
logic_issues: employee_unable_to_perform_job_functions contains a nested 'relationship == self' check inside conditions; relationship coverage belongs only in included_relationships, not inside condition objects

employee_unable_to_perform_job_functions is not a parallel qualifying pathway for what constitutes a serious health condition; it is a separate qualifying reason (825.112(a)(4)) that applies only to self and should be kept as a top-level condition but without the relationship sub-check
unsupported_conditions: The nested 'relationship == self' field inside employee_unable_to_perform_job_functions is not a condition on the health condition itself; it is relationship scoping and must be removed from conditions
format_issues: included_relationships uses 'child' instead of 'son or daughter' per 825.112(a)(3)
revision_summary: Changed 'child' to 'son or daughter' in included_relationships.required_value to match 825.112(a)(3) wording
Removed the nested 'relationship == self' check from employee_unable_to_perform_job_functions; relationship scoping belongs only in included_relationships
All other conditions retained as API1 had them correctly structured and grounded in the regulation chunks
